lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your brooch?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Yes! I thought I'd lost it. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Have one of these helmets. It will be useful if you want to work in the mine.

sections.title: Using a brooch on him again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your brooch?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: You returned my brooch earlier.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hello, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I sell you some ore?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Certainly. I will buy any iron or silver ore you mine. Speak to me again when you have some.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: I will give you [X] gold pieces for the ore you are carrying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do the Dorgeshuun live underground?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Our ancient legends say that goblins were created by an evil god in order to fight in a huge war. This god sent the Dorgeshuun tribe to fight a battle where all would die.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: But our ancestors escaped by hiding in a deep hole in the ground where our god could not find us. Eventually an earthquake sealed the hole and they were forever safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The war is over now, so you can return to the surface.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Even if the gods are no longer at war, I find it hard to believe that a tribe of peaceful goblins would be safe above. I have heard of humans coming down into the caves to slaughter our people!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: But even if we could return, we would not want to. We are adapted to living underground, and we have built a home for ourselves that we do not want to leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was the name of your god?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Our ancestors did not speak his name, thinking that to do so would attract his attention. His name has been unspoken for so long that it is now entirely forgotten.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: That is for the best. We have survived perfectly well without gods, and we do not want a return to the old ways.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to your arm?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: I lost it in a mining accident a few years ago.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: This area is very unstable. That's why we put markers on the wall showing the safest path.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you show me the way out of the mines?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Certainly. Come back soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you show me the way to the water mill cellar?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Certainly. Come back soon!
